summary: Vendored the upstream `doxygen-awesome-css` theme under `tools/doxygen-awesome-css` as a git subtree and removed the gen-pages package's runtime clone/symlink workflow. The PR mainly made documentation generation more self-contained by checking in the Doxygen theme assets, license, custom header/CSS/JS helpers, and local ignore rules. The body explicitly says this addressed earlier feedback from PR #629 by removing unused files; there were no review comments recorded on this PR itself.
what_changed: Added the `doxygen-awesome-css` theme assets under `tools/doxygen-awesome-css`, including the main CSS file, sidebar/dark-mode styles, JavaScript helpers, custom Doxygen header HTML, custom CSS, and an MIT license file.
Removed the `ln -s /opt/doxygen-awesome-css tools/` step from `.github/packages/gen-pages/entrypoint.sh` because the theme now lives in the repository.
Removed `tools/doxygen-awesome-css` from the root `.gitignore`, allowing the vendored subtree to be tracked.
Added a local `tools/doxygen-awesome-css/.gitignore` for upstream/editor/generated files such as `docs/html`, `.DS_Store`, and `.idea`.
The PR body notes that `Generate pages` would fail, but `Publish package (gen-pages)`, including its `Try generating pages` step, would pass.
smart_moves: Made docs generation less dependent on live network access and upstream repository availability by replacing a package-time clone with checked-in assets.
Kept third-party licensing explicit by adding the upstream `LICENSE` alongside the vendored files.
Removed the old root-level ignore entry so the subtree is visible to git, while keeping generated/editor noise ignored inside the vendored directory.
Simplified the gen-pages package setup and entrypoint by eliminating both the clone and the symlink.
Used a subtree-style vendor import rather than copying only an opaque CSS blob, which preserves a clear upstream package boundary under `tools/doxygen-awesome-css`.
decomp_lessons: For project documentation tooling, prefer hermetic/reproducible inputs over CI-time cloning when the assets are stable and small enough to vendor.
When vendoring third-party tooling assets, keep them in a clearly named `tools/<upstream-package>` directory and include the upstream license.
If an ignored path becomes intentionally tracked, remove the root `.gitignore` entry and move any generated-file ignores into the vendored subtree itself.
Documentation and packaging PRs can matter to decomp workflow even without touching matching code: reliable generated pages help contributors inspect headers, symbols, and cross-references.
When addressing prior review feedback from another PR, state the link and the concrete response in the body; here the body cites PR #629 review feedback and says unused files were removed.
naming_conventions: `tools/doxygen-awesome-css` mirrors the upstream repository/package name, making the vendored dependency searchable and recognizable.

The imported Doxygen Awesome JavaScript classes use upstream `DoxygenAwesome...` names such as `DoxygenAwesomeDarkModeToggle`, `DoxygenAwesomeFragmentCopyButton`, and `DoxygenAwesomeInteractiveToc`; these appear to be third-party names rather than melee-specific decomp symbols.
Custom Doxygen override files are grouped under `tools/doxygen-awesome-css/doxygen-custom/`, separating local theme customization from the main upstream assets.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Not applicable; this PR did not include decompilation matching, assembly, C source, headers, or symbol work.
No matching tactics can be inferred from the provided diff beyond general reproducible tooling practice.
review_feedback: No review comments or reviews are recorded in this PR slice.
The PR body says it addresses `https://github.com/doldecomp/melee/pull/629#pullrequestreview-1231162946` by removing unused files, but the exact prior reviewer wording is not present in this slice.
The body warns that `Generate pages` will fail while `Publish package (gen-pages)` and its `Try generating pages` step will pass; this appears to document expected CI behavior for reviewers.
follow_up_queries: Inspect PR #629 review comment `pullrequestreview-1231162946` to recover the exact unused-file feedback that motivated this subtree import.
Check later PRs touching `tools/doxygen-awesome-css` to see whether the subtree was updated from upstream or customized for melee docs.
